title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs ordering, single active consumer, and partitioning from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a keyed order and failover state machine in Go after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Partition related messages by stable business key while bounding skew, hot keys, resharding, and cross-key independence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use single active consumer when one active delivery stream is required and account for failover, prefetch, recovery, and throughput limits; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 4874 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Ordering Throughput Tradeoff trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, keep Ordering Throughput Tradeoff connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the review Ordering Scope Contract case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the review Ordering Scope Contract case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Ordering Throughput Tradeoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go messaging team handles case 4924 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieving Ordering Throughput Tradeoff through the concrete a keyed order and failover state machine in Go case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, keep Ordering Throughput Tradeoff connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Ordering Throughput Tradeoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 4924 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Ordering Throughput Tradeoff, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Strict global order can scale horizontally without coordination cost. In Go, one Go receive channel guarantees global business-event order.
